posts.caption:
Mommy bought me a sipper and now I refuse to let anyone hold it for me. 😌💧

A few weeks ago I didn’t know what water was, and now I’m sipping independently like a pro. 🥹

P.S. Every time I use this sipper, Mama gets asked where it’s from. 😂

If you’re starting your baby’s water journey, save this reel for later!

DAY 19 of solids with Avira 🍎🤍
This time we tried mashed apple ✨

Honestly, I wanted to slowly move towards slightly different textures now instead of making everything completely smooth in a blender 👶

✨ How I prepared it:
• Peeled the apple
• Cut it into small pieces
• Steamed it until soft
• Mashed it gently with a fork

She was making SO many faces while eating it 😂🥹
I genuinely thought she wasn’t liking it at all…

But surprisingly, she finished the entire bowl 🤍✨

Last time when I introduced apple on Day 10, I had blended it completely smooth in the blender. But this time, as we’re progressing in our solids journey, I wanted her to slowly experience a slightly coarser apple texture too 🍎

And honestly, this is something I’m learning every day — babies don’t always make expressions according to whether they like a food or not 😂
Sometimes they’re just exploring new tastes, temperatures & textures for the very first time.

✨ Benefits of apple for babies:
• Gentle on tiny tummies 🌱
• Helps digestion & gut health 🤍
• Rich in fiber ✨
• Supports immunity 🍎
• Naturally hydrating 💧
• Helps introduce new textures 👶
• Contains antioxidants for overall growth 💪

One thing I’m truly trying during this journey is not rushing into heavily flavored foods. I want Avira to slowly understand what each fruit, vegetable & grain naturally tastes like 🤍

DAY 17 & 18 of solids with Avira 🥣🤍
This time we tried plain oats porridge ✨

Honestly, I wanted to keep it SUPER simple with no fruits, sweeteners or flavoring added — just plain oats so she can slowly understand different textures and real flavors 👶

✨ How I prepared it:
• Took 2 tbsp oats
• Dry roasted them on a pan
• Let them cool & blended into powder
• Added the powder back to the pan with water
• Cooked it properly for 10–15 mins so it becomes easy to digest

I initially added 1 cup water, but it became slightly thick, so I added another cup to adjust the consistency 🤍

Also, I didn’t make it extremely runny because I want her to slowly explore different textures during this solids journey ✨

Why we repeated it for 2 days 👇
On Day 1, she didn’t really eat it properly, so I honestly couldn’t understand whether she liked it or not 🥹
That’s why we repeated it again the next day instead of instantly moving to another food.

On Day 2, she was a little gassy later in the day. We’re still not fully sure whether it was because of oats or something else because Day 1 was completely fine 🤍
So we’ll pause for a few days and then try oats again to understand better.

One thing motherhood is teaching me every day is that starting solids is literally trial, observation & patience 👶✨
Not every food works instantly, and that’s okay 🥹

✨ Benefits of oats for babies:
• Gentle on digestion 🌱
• Helps prevent constipation 💩
• Supports strong bones & teeth 🦴
• Boosts immunity ✨
• Gives sustained energy 💪
• Supports healthy growth & brain development 🧠

DAY 16 of solids with Avira 🥹🍠🤍

One day she was coughing, gagging, pushing the puree out…
and the very next day she was happily eating sweet potato 🥹

Honestly, this tiny experience taught me something so important about starting solids — babies are not just learning new foods, they’re learning textures, swallowing, chewing movements & how to eat for the very first time 👶✨

And sometimes, one tiny change can make ALL the difference.

✨ Sweet Potato Puree Recipe (6 months+)
• Peel & cut sweet potato into small pieces
• Steam until soft
• Mash with a fork instead of blending very runny
• Keep the texture smooth but slightly thicker so baby can slowly understand textures

One thing that REALLY helped us 👇
💧 Tiny sips of water in between some bites.

Sweet potato can feel sticky while swallowing, especially for beginners, and the water made her much more comfortable while eating 🤍

Also, this is your reminder that gagging during solids can be normal sometimes as babies learn how to manage food in their mouth. Of course, always supervise closely & follow safe feeding practices ✨

If your baby is also:
• rejecting foods
• coughing/gagging
• making faces
• or not eating much

Please know you’re not alone 🥹
Sometimes they just need repetition, patience & small adjustments.

This motherhood + solids journey is literally trial, error & learning together every single day 🤍

Don't forget to follow for PART 2 🤍

I genuinely thought she hated sweet potato after this reaction 🥹🍠

The coughing, gagging, pushing everything out… as a first-time mom, it honestly scared me so much. For a second, I thought maybe I introduced it too early or maybe she would never like it 😭

But slowly I’m realizing that starting solids is not just about “feeding food” to babies. They’re learning textures, learning how to swallow, learning completely new sensations for the very first time 🤍

And sometimes… one tiny change can make a huge difference.

If your baby is also rejecting a food, gagging, making faces or not eating properly — please know it doesn’t always mean they hate that food 🥹
Sometimes they just need time, repetition & a texture that feels comfortable to them.

Part 2 coming soon because the next day completely changed things for us 👀✨

DAY 13 & 14 of solids with Avira 🤍
Today we tried Moong Dal Khichdi with Lauki ✨

And honestly… this reel is proof that baby expressions can be SO confusing 😂
At moments, Avira looked like she was liking it… but she actually didn’t eat much and kept spitting most of it out 🥹

After a few bites, she wanted to breastfeed, so I offered milk instead 🤍
Maybe she wasn’t in the mood, maybe she didn’t enjoy the texture yet — and that’s okay. Starting solids is all about exposure, not perfection 👶✨

We still repeated it for 2 days because babies often need multiple tries before accepting a food. Since she still wasn’t very interested, we’ll pause and try again after a week or so 🤍

✨ Moong Dal Khichdi with Lauki Recipe (6 months+)
• 1 tbsp moong dal
• ½ tbsp rice
• Small pieces of peeled lauki
• 1 bowl water

Method:
• Soak rice & moong dal for 15–20 mins
• Add everything to a pressure cooker with water
• 1 whistle on high flame + 5 mins on low flame
• Once pressure releases completely, blend into a smooth puree consistency

Why this khichdi is amazing for babies 👇
✨ Moong dal is one of the easiest dals to digest
✨ Lauki is over 90% water & very hydrating
✨ Fiber helps prevent constipation naturally
✨ Rice + moong dal together support healthy growth & muscle development
✨ Lauki contains vitamins & minerals that support immunity & skin health
✨ Naturally cooling and gentle for summer months

This motherhood journey is teaching me that babies have their own tastes, moods & preferences… and sometimes “not today” simply means “try again later” 🥹🤍

From NICU wires & tiny newborn cries… to this smiling, healthy little girl today 🥹🤍

Sometimes I still look at her and cannot believe how far we’ve come.

She was born premature, so tiny, so fragile, and seeing my baby in the NICU was one of the hardest moments of my life. There were days filled with fear, tears, anxiety & endless prayers ✨

And now somehow… time has passed so quickly. 
The same baby who once fit so delicately in our hands is now laughing, playing, eating solids & filling our home with happiness every single day 🥹👶🏻

This journey has changed me completely as a mother. 

Some days were exhausting, emotional & overwhelming, but looking at her today makes every moment feel worth it.

To every mom of a premature baby reading this — I know how scary the beginning can feel. I know how heartbreaking it is seeing your baby so tiny & vulnerable. But babies are truly stronger than we can imagine 🫶🏻

They grow. 
They heal. 
They slowly catch up in their own beautiful time ✨

And one day you’ll look at them and wonder how they were ever that tiny in the first place 🥹🤍

DAY 12 of solids with Avira 🥦🤍 
Today’s menu: Broccoli Purée✨

The facial expressions? 10/10 dramatic 😂 
But she still managed to eat almost half the bowl and honestly… that’s a win for Day 12 🥹👏

✨ Easy Broccoli Puree Recipe (6 months+) 
• 2–3 broccoli florets (I have steamed more socan be used for evening meal)
• Steam until very soft 
• Blend with warm water/breastmilk/formula 
• Serve fresh and warm

Why broccoli is such a powerhouse for babies 👇 
🥦 Vitamin A supports healthy vision, growth & development 
🥦 Vitamin B6 supports brain development 
🥦 Fiber helps digestion & supports gut health 
🥦 Vitamin C helps immunity & improves iron absorption 
🥦 Potassium supports muscles, nerves & heart function 
🥦 Calcium helps build strong bones & teeth

One thing I’m learning in this motherhood journey: babies don’t need to LOVE a food on Day 1 🤍 
Sometimes they just need gentle exposure, patience, and repeated tries 🥹

Would your baby eat broccoli or make these same funny faces? 😂

Day 11 of Avira’s solids journey 🤍🥣

Today’s new food: Plain Sooji (Rava) Porridge ✨

One thing I’m learning in this motherhood journey is that simple foods are sometimes the best first foods for babies 🥹🤍

For Avira’s Sooji porridge, I kept everything extremely simple:
• 1.5 tbsp Sooji
• Around 2 bowls of water
• Cooked slowly on low flame till it became smooth, soft & easy to swallow 👶🏻✨

No ghee.
No flavoring like dates etc.

And that was intentional 🤍

Whenever I introduce a new food, I first like to offer it in its plain form so I can observe how Avira’s tummy responds to that one specific ingredient 🌼

If there’s any discomfort, allergy, or reaction, it becomes easier to identify the actual cause instead of getting confused between multiple ingredients 🫶🏻

Once she adjusts well to sooji, I’ll slowly start adding flavors like ghee and dates gradually ✨

Benefits of sooji for babies 👇🏻
🤍 Easy on tiny tummies
✨ Light & easy to digest
🧠 Supports brain development
🌼 Rich in iron & B vitamins
🥣 Great first cereal option
💪 Helps support healthy growth

Watching her explore new textures and flavors every single day is honestly becoming my favorite part of motherhood 😭🤍

Save this for your baby’s solids journey ✨
